cell_specs.implementation_plan: Run '!pip install -q numpy torch matplotlib ipywidgets anthropic' in a shell line. Then import: numpy as np, torch, torch.nn as nn, torch.nn.functional as F, matplotlib.pyplot as plt, ipywidgets as widgets, from ipywidgets import interact, from IPython.display import display HTML clear_output, import hashlib os time. Print versions: np.__version__, torch.__version__, widgets.__version__. Set matplotlib inline backend with '%matplotlib inline'.

Print a clear warning if torch is CPU-only ('CUDA not available — all attention ops will run on CPU, which is fine for this notebook').
test_checks: assert int(np.__version__.split('.')[0]) >= 1, 'numpy >= 1.x required'
print('All imports OK')

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define naive_attention_decode(token_embeddings, W_q, W_k, W_v). token_embeddings is np.ndarray (T, d), W_q/W_k/W_v are np.ndarray (d, d). Loop t in range(T): (1) Recompute K = token_embeddings[:t+1] @ W_k shape (t+1, d) and V = token_embeddings[:t+1] @ W_v shape (t+1, d) — this is the expensive recomputation. (2) q = token_embeddings[t] @ W_q shape (d,). (3) scores = q @ K.T / np.sqrt(d) shape (t+1,). (4) Apply max-subtraction for numerical stability: scores -= scores.max(). (5) weights = np.exp(scores); weights /= weights.sum() shape (t+1,). (6) output = weights @ V shape (d,). Append output. (7) Count ops: cumulative_ops += 2*(t+1) (2 full projection matmuls — K and V — each over t+1 tokens). Append cumulative_ops. Return (outputs, op_counts). After definition, run a demo with T=8, d=4, random seed 42, identity weights to show it works.
function_signatures: def naive_attention_decode(token_embeddings: np.ndarray, W_q: np.ndarray, W_k: np.ndarray, W_v: np.ndarray) -> tuple[list[np.ndarray], list[int]]
state_variables: naive_attention_decode (function, referenced by C05, C06, C07)
error_handling: Assert token_embeddings.ndim == 2, 'Expected (T, d) array'
Assert W_q.shape == W_k.shape == W_v.shape == (token_embeddings.shape[1], token_embeddings.shape[1]), 'Weight matrix shape mismatch'
Guard against weights summing to zero (should not happen with valid softmax but add assert weights.sum() > 1e-9)
test_checks: outputs, ops = naive_attention_decode(np.random.randn(8, 4), np.eye(4), np.eye(4), np.eye(4))
assert len(outputs) == 8 and len(ops) == 8, 'Output length must equal T'
assert outputs[0].shape == (4,), 'Each output must be shape (d,)'
print(f'Naive op counts: {ops}')  # Should grow as [2,6,12,20,30,42,56,72]

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define cached_attention_decode(token_embeddings, W_q, W_k, W_v). Same signature as C04. Initialize kv_cache_k = [] and kv_cache_v = [] (lists of np.ndarray). Loop t in range(T): (1) Compute ONLY the new token's projections: k_t = token_embeddings[t] @ W_k shape (d,), v_t = token_embeddings[t] @ W_v shape (d,). Append to caches. (2) K = np.stack(kv_cache_k) shape (t+1, d), V = np.stack(kv_cache_v) shape (t+1, d). (3) q = token_embeddings[t] @ W_q. (4) scores, weights, output same as C04. (5) cumulative_ops += 2 (only ONE new token projected for K and V). Append. Return (outputs, op_counts, (kv_cache_k, kv_cache_v)). After definition, run with SAME inputs as C04 demo (fix np.random.seed(42) before both), assert np.allclose on all outputs, and print the comparison.
function_signatures: def cached_attention_decode(token_embeddings: np.ndarray, W_q: np.ndarray, W_k: np.ndarray, W_v: np.ndarray) -> tuple[list[np.ndarray], list[int], tuple[list, list]]
error_handling: Same shape assertions as C04.
Assert len(kv_cache_k) == t+1 after each step (cache grows monotonically).
If np.allclose assertion fails, print both output arrays side by side for debugging before raising AssertionError.
test_checks: np.random.seed(42); emb = np.random.randn(8, 4); W = np.eye(4)
naive_out, naive_ops = naive_attention_decode(emb, W, W, W)
cached_out, cached_ops, _ = cached_attention_decode(emb, W, W, W)
for t, (n, c) in enumerate(zip(naive_out, cached_out)): assert np.allclose(n, c, atol=1e-6), f'Mismatch at step {t}'
assert cached_ops[-1] == 2 * 8, f'Cached total ops should be 2*T=16, got {cached_ops[-1]}'
print('Outputs match: True')
print(f'Cached op counts: {cached_ops}')  # Should be [2,4,6,8,10,12,14,16]

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Fix T=8. Build two 2D grids of shape (T, T) for visualization. naive_grid[step, pos] = 1 if pos <= step (red: recomputed from scratch), else 0 (white: not yet generated). cached_grid[step, pos]: 2 if pos == step (green: newly computed and cached this step), 1 if pos < step (light green: served from cache, no recompute), 0 if pos > step (white: future). Define a custom 3-color ListedColormap(['white', '#ff9999', '#cc0000']) for naive and (['white', '#99ddaa', '#00aa44']) for cached. Use plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5)). For each subplot: imshow with interpolation='nearest', set_xticks/yticks with labels 'Token 0'...'Token T-1' for columns and 'Step 0'...'Step T-1' for rows, add a text label in each colored cell ('R' for red recomputed cells in naive, 'C' for cached light-green cells, 'N' for new dark-green cells). Add colorbars with 3 categories. Title each plot clearly.
function_signatures: def build_naive_grid(T: int) -> np.ndarray
def build_cached_grid(T: int) -> np.ndarray
error_handling: Assert T >= 2 before building grids.
If matplotlib backend is not inline, call plt.show() explicitly.
test_checks: naive_grid = build_naive_grid(8); assert naive_grid.shape == (8, 8)
assert naive_grid[0, 0] == 1 and naive_grid[0, 1] == 0, 'At step 0, only position 0 is colored'
assert naive_grid[7, 7] == 1 and np.sum(naive_grid[7]) == 8, 'At step 7, all 8 positions are recomputed'
cached_grid = build_cached_grid(8); assert cached_grid[3, 3] == 2 and cached_grid[3, 2] == 1 and cached_grid[3, 4] == 0

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define inner function plot_flop_comparison(T). Inside: set np.random.seed(0); d=16 (small for speed); emb = np.random.randn(T, d); W = np.eye(d). Call naive_attention_decode(emb, W, W, W) → naive_ops. Call cached_attention_decode(emb, W, W, W) → cached_ops. Create plt.figure(figsize=(9, 4)). Plot steps=list(range(1,T+1)) on x-axis. Line 1: naive_ops in red with label f'Naive O(T²) — {naive_ops[-1]} total ops'. Line 2: cached_ops in green with label f'KV Cache O(T) — {cached_ops[-1]} total ops'. Fill between the two curves with alpha=0.15 red. Add xlabel 'Generation Step', ylabel 'Cumulative K/V Projection Ops', title f'KV Cache vs Naive at T={T}', legend, tight_layout, plt.show(). Print speedup ratio. Wrap the function in interact() with IntSlider(min=4, max=256, step=4, value=32, description='Sequence length T', continuous_update=False).
function_signatures: def plot_flop_comparison(T: int) -> None
error_handling: Use continuous_update=False on the slider to avoid triggering on every drag tick (which would be slow).
Wrap plt.close('all') before plt.figure() to prevent figure accumulation in Colab.
test_checks: plot_flop_comparison(16)  # Run once without interact to confirm no errors
Visually verify: naive curve is roughly parabolic, cached curve is a straight line with much lower slope.

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define module-level DTYPE_BYTES = {'FP32': 4, 'FP16': 2, 'INT8': 1, 'BF16': 2}. Define kv_cache_bytes_per_token(num_layers, num_kv_heads, head_dim, dtype_bytes): return num_layers * num_kv_heads * head_dim * dtype_bytes * 2. The factor 2 accounts for both K and V. Define max_tokens(gpu_memory_gb, model_weight_gb, num_layers, num_kv_heads, head_dim, dtype_bytes): available = (gpu_memory_gb - model_weight_gb) * (1024**3); bpt = kv_cache_bytes_per_token(...); return int(available / bpt) if bpt > 0 else 0. Run demonstration: Gemma 2 27B has num_layers=46, num_kv_heads=16, head_dim=128, dtype='FP16'. NOTE: The course slide uses num_heads=32 (query heads) in its worked example to get 753,664 bytes; our formula correctly uses num_kv_heads=16 (the actual KV cache cost for GQA), giving 376,832 bytes. Print both values with explanation. model_weight_gb = 54 (27B params × 2 bytes ≈ 54 GB). max_tok = max_tokens(80, 54, 46, 16, 128, 2).
function_signatures: def kv_cache_bytes_per_token(num_layers: int, num_kv_heads: int, head_dim: int, dtype_bytes: int) -> int
def max_tokens(gpu_memory_gb: float, model_weight_gb: float, num_layers: int, num_kv_heads: int, head_dim: int, dtype_bytes: int) -> int
state_variables: DTYPE_BYTES (dict, used by C10, C11, C15, C16)
kv_cache_bytes_per_token (function, used by C10, C11, C15, C16)
error_handling: Raise ValueError if model_weight_gb >= gpu_memory_gb: 'Model weights exceed GPU memory; no room for KV cache'.
Return 0 (not error) if dtype_bytes is 0 or any dimension is 0 — caller should check.
Print a clear note distinguishing num_kv_heads (GQA) from num_q_heads (query heads) to address the slide's different calculation.
test_checks: assert kv_cache_bytes_per_token(1, 1, 1, 2) == 4, 'Minimal case: 1 layer, 1 head, dim=1, FP16 = 2*2=4 bytes'
gemma_bpt = kv_cache_bytes_per_token(46, 16, 128, 2)
assert gemma_bpt == 46 * 16 * 128 * 2 * 2, f'Expected {46*16*128*2*2}, got {gemma_bpt}'
print(f'Gemma 2 27B (GQA, num_kv_heads=16): {gemma_bpt:,} bytes = {gemma_bpt/1024:.1f} KB per token')
print(f'Max tokens on A100-80GB: {max_tokens(80, 54, 46, 16, 128, 2):,}')

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define three classes inheriting nn.Module. Common config: d_model=64, num_q_heads=8, head_dim=d_model//num_q_heads=8. (1) MultiHeadAttention: W_q=Linear(64,64), W_k=Linear(64,64), W_v=Linear(64,64), W_o=Linear(64,64). forward(x: Tensor shape (T,64)): unsqueeze batch dim → (1,T,64). Q=W_q(x).view(1,T,8,8).transpose(1,2) → (1,8,T,8). Same for K, V with W_k, W_v. scores=Q@K.mT/sqrt(8) → (1,8,T,T). weights=softmax(scores,-1). out=weights@V → (1,8,T,8). Reshape to (1,T,64). W_o(out).squeeze(0) → (T,64). Store self.kv_cache=(K,V). (2) MultiQueryAttention: W_k=Linear(64,8) and W_v=Linear(64,8) (single head dimension). K=W_k(x).unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0) → (1,1,T,8) then unsqueeze to match (1,1,T,8). Broadcast against Q (1,8,T,8). (3) GroupedQueryAttention(num_kv_groups=2): W_k=Linear(64, 2*8=16), W_v=Linear(64,16). K=W_k(x).view(1,T,2,8).transpose(1,2) → (1,2,T,8). K_expanded=K.repeat_interleave(4, dim=1) → (1,8,T,8). After all class definitions, instantiate all three with torch.manual_seed(0), run forward on x=torch.randn(6,64), print output.shape and kv_cache shapes.
function_signatures: class MultiHeadAttention(nn.Module): def __init__(self, d_model=64, num_q_heads=8)
class MultiHeadAttention(nn.Module): def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor
class MultiQueryAttention(nn.Module): def __init__(self, d_model=64, num_q_heads=8)
class MultiQueryAttention(nn.Module): def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor
class GroupedQueryAttention(nn.Module): def __init__(self, d_model=64, num_q_heads=8, num_kv_groups=2)
class GroupedQueryAttention(nn.Module): def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor
error_handling: In GroupedQueryAttention.__init__, assert num_q_heads % num_kv_groups == 0, f'num_q_heads={num_q_heads} must be divisible by num_kv_groups={num_kv_groups}'
In forward, if x.dim() == 2 add batch dim via x.unsqueeze(0); if x.dim() == 3 use as-is. Always squeeze batch at output.
Use torch.no_grad() context in forward during demo to avoid grad overhead.
test_checks: torch.manual_seed(0); x = torch.randn(6, 64)
mha = MultiHeadAttention(); mqa = MultiQueryAttention(); gqa = GroupedQueryAttention()
with torch.no_grad(): o_mha = mha(x); o_mqa = mqa(x); o_gqa = gqa(x)
assert o_mha.shape == (6, 64), f'MHA output shape wrong: {o_mha.shape}'
assert o_mqa.shape == (6, 64), f'MQA output shape wrong: {o_mqa.shape}'
assert o_gqa.shape == (6, 64), f'GQA output shape wrong: {o_gqa.shape}'
assert mha.kv_cache[0].shape == (1, 8, 6, 8), 'MHA K cache should be (1, num_q_heads, T, head_dim)'
assert mqa.kv_cache[0].shape == (1, 1, 6, 8), 'MQA K cache should be (1, 1, T, head_dim)'
assert gqa.kv_cache[0].shape == (1, 2, 6, 8), 'GQA K cache should be (1, num_kv_groups, T, head_dim)'
print('All attention variants produce correct output shapes.')

Set ax.set_xlabel('Token Position'), ax.set_ylabel('Query Head Index'), ax.set_title(f'name (num_kv_heads={num_kv_heads})\nKV cache = {kv_cache_bytes_per_token(32, num_kv_heads, 128, 2)//1024} KB/token'). Add a custom legend using matplotlib Patch for each unique KV head color. After all three panels, add colorbar on last axis. plt.suptitle, plt.tight_layout, plt.show.

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define class PrefixCacheSimulator with class-level constants FRESH_COST = 2.50/1_000_000 and CACHED_COST = 0.25/1_000_000 (dollars per token). __init__: self.cache = {} (maps hashlib.md5(prefix_str).hexdigest() → list of tokens). _tokenize(text): return text.lower().split(). _hash(tokens): return hashlib.md5(' '.join(tokens).encode()).hexdigest(). process(system_prompt, user_message): (1) sys_toks = _tokenize(system_prompt), user_toks = _tokenize(user_message), all_toks = sys_toks + user_toks. (2) Find longest cached prefix: iterate i from len(sys_toks) down to 1; for each prefix = sys_toks[:i], compute h=_hash(prefix); if h in self.cache, set hit_len=i, break; else hit_len=0. (3) Store _hash(sys_toks) → sys_toks in self.cache. (4) miss_len = len(all_toks) - hit_len. cost = hit_len*CACHED_COST + miss_len*FRESH_COST. (5) Return dict: total_tokens, hit_tokens=hit_len, miss_tokens=miss_len, cost, tokens=all_toks, hit_mask=[True]*hit_len+[False]*miss_len. After class definition, create sim=PrefixCacheSimulator(), define SYSTEM_PROMPT and USER1, USER2. Run r1=sim.process(SYSTEM_PROMPT, USER1) and r2=sim.process(SYSTEM_PROMPT, USER2). Print formatted breakdown for both. Print cost saving: r1['cost']+r2_full_cost - r1['cost'] - r2['cost'].
function_signatures: class PrefixCacheSimulator:
def __init__(self) -> None
def _tokenize(self, text: str) -> list[str]
def _hash(self, tokens: list[str]) -> str
def process(self, system_prompt: str, user_message: str) -> dict
state_variables: PrefixCacheSimulator (class, used by C19, C20-simulation path)
SYSTEM_PROMPT (str, default system prompt, also used as default in C19)
error_handling: If system_prompt is empty string, hit_len=0 and cache entire prompt as miss.
If user_message is empty, process just the system prompt tokens — do not crash.
If self.cache is very large (>10k entries), print a warning but do not evict — this is a toy simulator.
test_checks: sim = PrefixCacheSimulator()
r1 = sim.process('Hello world from the system', 'user says hi')
assert r1['hit_tokens'] == 0, 'First call must be all cache miss'
assert r1['total_tokens'] == len('Hello world from the system user says hi'.split())
r2 = sim.process('Hello world from the system', 'user says bye')
assert r2['hit_tokens'] == len('Hello world from the system'.split()), 'Second call: system prompt should be full cache hit'
assert r2['cost'] < r1['cost'], 'Second call must be cheaper due to cache hit'
print(f'Test passed. Second call savings: ${(r1["cost"]-r2["cost"])*1e6:.2f} per million calls')

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define DEFAULT_SYSTEM as a multi-line string (~5 sentences describing an AI travel agent with tools and instructions). Define DEFAULT_USER1='Book a flight from Taipei to Boston for next Monday' and DEFAULT_USER2='Book a flight from San Francisco to New York for Friday' (matching the slide 25 example). Create widgets: sys_area=widgets.Textarea(value=DEFAULT_SYSTEM, description='System:', layout=Layout(width='95%',height='100px')); user1_area=widgets.Textarea(value=DEFAULT_USER1, description='User 1:', layout=Layout(width='95%')); user2_area=widgets.Textarea(value=DEFAULT_USER2, description='User 2:', layout=Layout(width='95%')); btn=widgets.Button(description='Compute Cache Hits', button_style='primary', icon='search'); out=widgets.Output(). Define on_click(b): fresh PrefixCacheSimulator each click. Run r1=sim.process(sys_area.value, user1_area.value) then r2=sim.process(sys_area.value, user2_area.value). Build HTML for each result: wrap each token in a <span> with background #d4edda (green) if hit_mask[i] else #ffffff (white). Include padded border, monospace font. Below token strip: show hit rate % and estimated cost. Display HTML in out widget. Call display(VBox([sys_area, user1_area, user2_area, btn, out])).
function_signatures: def on_click(b: widgets.Button) -> None
def tokens_to_html(tokens: list[str], hit_mask: list[bool], label: str, hit_rate: float, cost: float, savings: float) -> str
state_variables: DEFAULT_SYSTEM, DEFAULT_USER1, DEFAULT_USER2 (str, default widget values)
error_handling: In on_click, wrap entire body in try/except Exception as e: with out: clear_output(); print(f'Error: {e}') to prevent silent widget failures.
If system prompt textarea is blank, display a red warning HTML message instead of computing.
Call clear_output(wait=True) before display to prevent output stacking on repeated button clicks.
test_checks: Manually trigger on_click with default values and verify green tokens appear for the system prompt portion of Request 2.
Verify hit rate for Request 1 is 0% (cold cache) and Request 2 hit rate equals len(DEFAULT_SYSTEM.split()) / (len(DEFAULT_SYSTEM.split()) + len(DEFAULT_USER2.split())) * 100.

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Define LONG_SYSTEM_PROMPT as a multi-line string of approximately 500 words describing an ML expert assistant (covering topics: transformers, attention, KV cache, inference optimization, hardware). Define SHORT_USER = 'What is the key benefit of KV Cache in one sentence?'. Define measure_ttft(client, system_prompt, user_message) using the Anthropic streaming API: start=time.perf_counter(); open context manager 'with client.messages.stream(model=claude-sonnet-4-6, max_tokens=50, system=system_prompt, messages=[{role:user, content:user_message}]) as stream:'; iterate stream text_stream; on first chunk set first_token_time=time.perf_counter() and break. Return first_token_time - start. Main block: try: import anthropic; api_key=os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'); if not api_key: raise ValueError('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set'). client=anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key). Print 'Measuring cold TTFT...'; ttft_cold=measure_ttft(client, LONG_SYSTEM_PROMPT, SHORT_USER). time.sleep(0.5). Print 'Measuring warm TTFT (cached prefix)...'; ttft_warm=measure_ttft(client, LONG_SYSTEM_PROMPT, SHORT_USER). Print results table. except (ImportError, ValueError) as e: print('API key not set — skipping live demo; see C18–C19 for simulation'). print(f'Reason: {e}').
function_signatures: def measure_ttft(client: 'anthropic.Anthropic', system_prompt: str, user_message: str) -> float
state_variables: LONG_SYSTEM_PROMPT (str, ~500 words, stable prefix for caching demo)
error_handling: Catch anthropic.APIConnectionError and print 'Connection failed — check your network and API key' before skipping.
Catch anthropic.RateLimitError and print 'Rate limit hit — wait 60s and retry'.
If ttft_cold or ttft_warm is 0 (stream returned nothing), print warning and skip speedup calculation.
Add a note that TTFT variance is high in practice; run 3 times and average for reliable measurement.
test_checks: assert len(LONG_SYSTEM_PROMPT.split()) >= 400, 'System prompt too short to exercise prefix caching'
If API key is set: assert ttft_cold > 0 and ttft_warm > 0
If API key is set: assert ttft_cold >= ttft_warm * 0.5, 'Warm TTFT should not be slower than cold (within 2× variance)'
print('Live API demo complete.' if api_key else 'Live demo skipped — simulation cells C18-C19 demonstrate the concept.')

cell_specs.implementation_plan: Import pandas as pd (already in C02). Define summary_data as a list of dicts with keys: Method, Approach, Changes_Attention, Needs_Retraining, Other_Cost. Rows: ('Flash Attention', 'Reduce HBM↔SRAM data movement', 'No', 'No', 'Extra compute + implementation complexity'), ('KV Cache', 'Store computed K/V for reuse at each step', 'No', 'No', 'GPU memory usage grows with context'), ('Multi-query Attention', 'All query heads share one K/V pair', 'Yes', 'Yes', 'May significantly hurt model quality'), ('Group-query Attention', 'Groups of queries share K/V pairs', 'Yes', 'Yes', 'Minor quality impact; used in Llama/Gemma'), ('MLA (DeepSeek)', 'Compress K/V to low-rank latent vector', 'Yes', 'Yes', 'Extra projection matrices'), ('Sliding Window Attention', 'Limit attention to W most recent tokens', 'Yes', '?', 'Cannot attend past window boundary'), ('Streaming LLM', 'Window + attention sink tokens', 'Yes', '?', 'Must always keep first few tokens'), ('Pruning KV Cache', 'Evict low-attention K/V entries', 'Yes', 'No', 'May significantly hurt model quality'). df = pd.DataFrame(summary_data). Rename columns to display-friendly names. Define color_yn(val): return green bg+text for 'No', red bg+text for 'Yes', yellow for '?', else ''. Apply .applymap(color_yn, subset=['Changes Attention?','Needs Retraining?']). Apply .set_table_styles with header styling. Display styled df.
function_signatures: def color_yn(val: str) -> str
error_handling: If pd.io.formats.style is unavailable (rare edge case), fall back to df.to_string() with print.
Use .applymap (pandas < 2.1) or .map (pandas >= 2.1) — check pd.__version__ and use appropriate method to avoid DeprecationWarning.
test_checks: assert len(summary_data) == 8, 'Summary table must have 8 rows'
assert all(k in summary_data[0] for k in ['Method','Approach','Changes_Attention','Needs_Retraining','Other_Cost'])
kv_row = next(r for r in summary_data if r['Method'] == 'KV Cache')
assert kv_row['Changes_Attention'] == 'No' and kv_row['Needs_Retraining'] == 'No', 'KV Cache should require no attention changes and no retraining'
print('Summary table rendered successfully.')

cross_cell_invariants: All code cells assume C02 has been executed first; any cell run in isolation will raise NameError. Add a guard comment '# Run C02 first' at the top of each code cell.
numpy random seed must be fixed to np.random.seed(42) before any random array generation in C04/C05/C07 to ensure reproducibility across re-runs.
kv_cache_bytes_per_token from C09 is the single source of truth for all memory calculations in C10, C11, C15, C16. No cell should reimplement this formula independently.
The factor of 2 in kv_cache_bytes_per_token (for K and V) must never be 3 (K, Q, V) — Q is not cached.
All matplotlib figures must call plt.close('all') before plt.figure() or plt.subplots() to prevent figure accumulation in long Colab sessions.
All widget interact callbacks must use continuous_update=False to avoid triggering expensive computations on every slider tick.
PrefixCacheSimulator from C18 must be instantiated fresh inside on_click in C19 (not reused across clicks), so cache state reflects exactly the two requests shown — not prior click history.
C20 is the only cell requiring external credentials; its try/except guard must print a human-readable skip message that references C18–C19 as the fallback.
pandas must be imported in C02 (add 'import pandas as pd') even though it is only used in C16 and C22 — consistent with the single-import-cell convention.
All attention implementations in C13 must store kv_cache as an instance attribute (self.kv_cache) set during forward(), not __init__(), so the cache reflects the most recent forward pass.
assumptions: Colab runtime is Python 3.10+ with standard Colab pre-installed packages (numpy, torch, matplotlib, pandas, ipywidgets); only 'anthropic' requires explicit pip install.
ipywidgets requires '%load_ext widgetsnbextension' or equivalent in Colab — this is already enabled by default in recent Colab; no extra setup cell is needed.
The word-split tokenizer in C18/C19 is intentionally naive (str.split) — it is sufficient to demonstrate prefix matching without requiring tiktoken or sentencepiece.
PyTorch attention in C13 uses causal attention (full attention over all T positions) for simplicity — a causal mask is not added, as the pedagogical focus is on KV sharing structure not masking.
The TTFT measurement in C20 includes network round-trip time; the demo illustrates relative speedup (warm vs cold) rather than absolute TTFT values.

Gemma 2 27B parameter counts: num_kv_heads=16 is used (GQA correct value), not 32 (query heads); this intentionally differs from the slide's worked example which used query heads — both values are printed and explained.

The summary table in C22 uses '?' for 'Needs Retraining?' for Sliding Window Attention and Streaming LLM, matching slide 27, because these techniques are sometimes applied via fine-tuning and sometimes via inference-time heuristics only.
